steps.task_description:
Design a token-bucket HTTP rate limiter for the FastAPI gateway.

Constraints:
  - Per-IP, per-API-key, and global ceilings.
  - Pluggable backend (memory + redis).
  - All public symbols typed (3.12+ syntax).

Emit ONLY the interface + file layout:
  - rate_limiter/__init__.py  (re-exports)
  - rate_limiter/types.py     (protocols + dataclasses)
  - rate_limiter/buckets.py   (bucket interface + memory impl)
  - rate_limiter/middleware.py (FastAPI middleware adapter)

Publish a KNOWLEDGE_SHARE(domain_tag=code_patterns,
knowledge_type=draft_interface, content=...) so peer
implementers and reviewers can pick it up.

Audit the project's pyproject.toml for any new direct or
transitive dependencies the rate limiter might pull in
(redis client, prometheus_client).  Cross-check the latest
compatible versions against known CVEs and report any
license incompatibilities.

Implement the rate limiter modules from the architect's
draft_interface (read from the cluster scratchpad).  Slice
the work across cluster members:

  member 1: rate_limiter/types.py + __init__.py
  member 2: rate_limiter/buckets.py
  member 3: rate_limiter/middleware.py

Each member writes the canonical implementation back to
the scratchpad under
  acc:<cid>:cluster:<cluster_id>:impl:<filename>
and emits a KNOWLEDGE_SHARE(knowledge_type=impl_ready,
content=<scratchpad key>) on completion.

Generate pytest cases for every public symbol of the
rate limiter, organised one test module per source file.
Cover the positive case, a boundary case, and at least
one negative case per public API.  Include a fixture
that swaps in the memory backend so tests do not require
Redis.

After generation, run the suite via [SKILL: test_execution]
and emit an EVAL_OUTCOME against the role's eval_rubric so
the arbiter can rank the cluster's outputs.

Read the implementer output (scratchpad under cluster
keys) plus the tester verdict.  Produce a single
JSON verdict: PASS | NEEDS_CHANGES | FAIL.  HIGH or
CRITICAL security findings MUST trigger ALERT_ESCALATE
before this step completes.

Specifically check:
  - Is the architect's draft_interface contract honoured?
  - Are there missing test cases the tester verdict admits?
  - Any hidden Cat-A risks (skill or MCP outside allow-list,
    unbounded resource use)?
